etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kak. Cognate to Kyrgyz как (kak), etc.

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *ōn (“ten”). Cognate with Kazakh он (on), Kumyk он (on), Azerbaijani on Turkish on, Shor он, etc.

etymology_text: Compare to Azerbaijani çay.
From Persian چای (čây), from Sinitic 茶 (MC drae, “tea”).
From Proto-Turkic *arpa (“barley”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Proto-Turkic *beŕ. Cognate with
Kazakh без (bez, “gland”),
Karachay-Balkar без (bez, “gland”),
Kumyk без (bez, “gland”),
Uzbek bez (“gland”),
Uyghur بەز (bez, “gland”),
Turkmen mäz (“gland”),
Bashkir биҙ (biź, “gland”),
Tuvan бес (bes, “gland”),
Chuvash пар (par, “gland”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Borrowed from Arabic طَعْم (ṭaʕm). Cognate with Kazakh дәм (däm), Kyrgyz даам (daam), Bashkir тәм (təm), Tatar тәм (täm), Turkmen tagam, Uzbek taʻm, Uyghur تەم (tem), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic.
Borrowed from Russian май (maj).
From Proto-Turkic *bań. cognate with Kazakh май (mai), Kyrgyz май (may), Crimean Tatar may, Kumyk май (may), Tatar май (may), Uzbek moy, etc.
From Proto-Common Turkic *taz, Proto-Turkic *tāŕ. Cognate to Crimean Tatar taz, Kazakh таз (taz), Azerbaijani daz, Salar daz (“bald”), etc.

glosses: bald spot, bald patch; bald head

etymology_text: From Proto-Common Turkic *taz, Proto-Turkic *tāŕ. Cognate to Crimean Tatar taz, Kazakh таз (taz), Azerbaijani daz, Salar daz (“bald”), etc.

From Proto-Turkic *nē-.
Cognate with Kazakh не (ne), Karakalpak ne, Nogai не (ne, “what?”), Kyrgyz не (ne), Crimean Tatar ne, Kumyk не (ne), Bashkir ни (ni), Tatar ни (ni), Azerbaijani nə, Salar neñ, Turkish ne (“what?”), Uzbek na, Uyghur نە (ne), Khakas ноо (noo), Shor ноо, Tuvan чүү (çüü), Western Yugur ne, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Persian توت (tut).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Russian карандаш (karandaš).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Persian بازار (bâzâr).
From Proto-Turkic *bas-. Compare to Kumyk басмакъ (basmaq), Kyrgyz басуу (basuu), etc.
From Proto-Turkic *bal (“honey”). Cognate with Kazakh бал (bal), Kyrgyz бал (bal), Crimean Tatar bal, Kumyk бал (bal), Azerbaijani bal, Turkish bal, Shor пал, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Proto-Turkic *baĺmak (“boot, shoe”).
Compare Kumyk башмакъ (başmaq), Tatar башмак (başmaq), Turkish başmak, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *bok. Cognate to Kyrgyz бок (bok), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *bār. Kazakh бар (bar), Kyrgyz бар (bar), Crimean Tatar bar, Kumyk бар (bar), Bashkir бар (bar), Tatar бар (bar), Azerbaijani var, Turkish var, Uzbek bor, Uyghur بار (bar), etc.

glosses: there is / there are, there exist; I/you have, he/she has, etc.

etymology_text: Compare Kyrgyz баары (baarı), Kazakh бәрі (bärı), Kumyk бары (barı), etc.

From Proto-Turkic *ōt (“fire”). Cognate with Kazakh от (ot), Kyrgyz от (ot), Crimean Tatar ot, Kumyk от (ot), Azerbaijani od, Turkish od, Khakas от (ot), Shor от, Tuvan от (ot), Western Yugur ot, etc.

From Proto-Turkic *ot (“grass”). Cognate with Kazakh от (ot), Kyrgyz от (ot), Crimean Tatar ot, Kumyk от (ot), Azerbaijani ot, Turkish ot, Khakas от (ot), Shor от, Tuvan оът (òt), Western Yugur ot/ uʰt, etc.

glosses: The sixth letter of the Southern Altai alphabet, written in the Cyrillic script.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Borrowed from Russian сыр (syr, “cheese”).
Compare to Kyrgyz сыр (sır, “dye, paint”)
From Proto-Turkic *yūrt (“dwelling place, home”). Cognate with Kyrgyz журт (jurt), etc.

From Proto-Turkic *yōk. Cognate with Kazakh жоқ (joq), Kyrgyz жок (jok), Crimean Tatar yoq, Kumyk ёкъ (yoq), Tatar юк (yuq), Azerbaijani yox, Turkish yok, Turkmen ýok, Khakas чох (çox), Shor чоқ, Tuvan чок (çok), Western Yugur yoq, etc.

From Proto-Turkic *yōk. Cognate with Kazakh жоқ (joq), Kyrgyz жок (jok), Crimean Tatar yoq, Kumyk ёкъ (yoq), Tatar юк (yuq), Azerbaijani yox, Turkish yok, Turkmen ýok, Khakas чох (çox), Shor чоқ, Tuvan чок (çok), Western Yugur yoq, etc.
From Proto-Turkic *ïm.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
Perhaps from a Mongolic source - compare Mongolian чуулган (čuulgan, “a gathering”), Buryat суглаан (suglaan, “to gather”).
Compare to
Tatar чолан (çolan, “anteroom”),
Bashkir солан (solan),
Kazakh шолан (şolan, “anteroom”);
Shor шулан (“cattle yard”).
From Proto-Turkic *bor (“chalk”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *āla. Cognate to Kyrgyz ала (ala), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kur (“belt”). Cognate with Kazakh құр (qūr), Kyrgyz кур (kur), Shor қур, Western Yugur qur, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *sān. Cognate with Kazakh сан (san), Kyrgyz сан (san), Crimean Tatar san, Kumyk сан (san, “limp”), Tatar сан (san, “limp”), etc.
From Proto-Turkic *sā-n. Cognate to Kyrgyz сан (san), Kazakh сан (san), Kumyk сан (san), etc.
From Proto-Turkic *ata.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *sōl (“left”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kobga. Cognate to Turkish kova, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Russian исто́рия (istórija).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *tuman.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *siŋek.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Ultimately from Persian صندوق (sanduq).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From *qatïq, from a suffix-derived noun from the well-attested verbal stem *qat- "to add; season a dish". Analyzable as кату (katu) + -ык (-ïk). Cognate with
Bashkir ҡатыҡ (qatıq),
Kyrgyz катык (katık),
Kazakh қатық (qatyq),
Uzbek qatiq,
Uyghur قېتىق (qëtiq),
Crimean Tatar qatıq,
Turkmen gatyk,
Azerbaijani qatıq (“creamy dairy product”),
etc.

glosses: qatiq (thick soured milk product, more solid form of yogurt)

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *būt (“leg, foot”). Cognate with Turkish but (“thigh”).

Cognate to Azerbaijani adaş, Bashkir аҙаш (aźaş), Kazakh адас (adas) and аттас (attas), Kyrgyz атташ (attaş), Turkish adaş, Tatar адаш (adaş), Turkmen adaş, Tuvan атташ (attaş), Uyghur ئاداش (adash), Uzbek adash.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *adgïr (“stallion”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *āy (“moon, month”).

Southern Altai ак
Inherited from Proto-Turkic *āk.

etymology_text: From *aqča, Cognate with Kazakh ақша (aqşa), Kyrgyz акча (akca), Crimean Tatar aqça, Kumyk акъча (aqça), Turkish akçe, Shor ақча, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
Cognate with
Tatar алабуга (alabuğa, “perch”),
Kazakh алабұға (alabūğa, “perch”),
Kyrgyz алабуга (alabuga, “trout”),
Shor алабуға (“perch”),
Khakas ала пуға (ala puğa, “perch”),
Uzbek olabugʻa (“perch”),
Uyghur ئالابۇغا (alabugha, “perch”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *karsak. Cognate to Kyrgyz карсак (karsak), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *d(i)ār (“narrow”). Cognate with Kazakh тар (tar), Kyrgyz тар (tar), Crimean Tatar tar, Kumyk тар (tar), Azerbaijani dar, Turkish dar, Shor тар, Western Yugur tar, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *siāŕ.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *sag. Compare to Kyrgyz соо (soo).
From Proto-Turkic *sag. Compare to Kyrgyz соо (soo).
As a unit of currency, the Turkic root *som literally means pure, implying pure gold.
As a unit of currency, the Turkic root *som literally means pure, implying pure gold.
From Arabic شَرْط (šarṭ).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kulkak (“ear”). Cognate with Kazakh құлақ (qūlaq), Kyrgyz кулак (kulak), Crimean Tatar qulaq, Karachay-Balkar къулакъ (qulaq), Kumyk къулакъ (qulaq), Bashkir ҡолаҡ (qolaq), Tatar колак (qolaq), Azerbaijani qulaq, Gagauz kulak, Turkish kulak, Turkmen gulak, Uzbek quloq, Uyghur قۇلاق (qulaq), Khakas хулах (xulax), Shor қулақ, Tofa ӄулаӄ, Tuvan кулак (kulak), Western Yugur qulaq, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
Borrowed from Middle Russian ᲅꙋра (tura, “tower, castle”), from Middle French tour (“tower”), from Old French tor (“tower”), from Latin turrim, accusative of turris (“tower”), from Ancient Greek τύρρις (túrrhis), alternant of τύρσις (túrsis, “tower”).
Compare also Siberian Tatar тора (“city, town”), Tatar тора (tora, “tempe; city, town”); Shor тура (tura, “city, town”), Khakas тура (tura, “house; city, town”), Buryat тура (tura, “house; city, town”), etc

etymology_templates.name: bor+
etymology_templates.expansion: Borrowed from Middle Russian ᲅꙋра (tura, “tower, castle”)

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *tōn. Cognates with Kazakh тон (ton), Kyrgyz тон (ton), Crimean Tatar don (“pants”), Kumyk тон (ton), Turkish don, Khakas тон (ton), Shor тон, Western Yugur ton, etc.
From Proto-Turkic *ōδ.
From Proto-Turkic *ōj.
From Proto-Turkic *tōk. Cognate to Kyrgyz ток (tok), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *altï (“six”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Proto-Turkic *ūn (“flour”).
Cognate with Kazakh ұн (ūn, “flour”), Kyrgyz ун (un, “flour”), Uzbek un (“flour”), Crimean Tatar un, Kumyk ун (un), Bashkir он (on), Khakas ун (un, “flour”), Shor ун,Azerbaijani un, Turkish un (“flour”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Proto-Turkic *kïrk (“forty”).
Cognate with Kazakh қырық (qyryq), Kyrgyz кырк (kırk), Crimean Tatar qırq, Karachay-Balkar къыркъ (qırq), Kumyk къыркъ (qırq), Bashkir ҡырҡ (qırq), Azerbaijani qırx, Turkish kırk, Turkmen kyrk, Uzbek qirq, Khakas хырых (xırıx), Shor қырық, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *altmïĺ. Cognate to Kyrgyz алтымыш (altımış), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *bīr (“one”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kīĺ (“sable”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From алты (altï) + -нчы (-nčï). Cognates include Azerbaijani altıncı, Turkish altıncı, Turkmen altynjy, Kazakh алтыншы (altynşy), Kyrgyz алтынчы (altıncı), Crimean Tatar altıncı, Kumyk алтынчы (altınçı), Bashkir алтынсы (altınsı), Tatar алтынчы (altınçı),
Uzbek oltinchi, Uyghur ئالتىنچى (altinchi), Khakas алтынӌы (altıncı), Shor алтынчы (altïnčï), Northern Altai алтынджы (altïndžï) / алтынчы (altïnčï), Western Yugur altïnč, Yakut алтыс (altïs).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Common Turkic *aš (“food, meal”), from Proto-Turkic *(i)aĺ (“food, meal”).
Cognate with
Old Uyghur [script needed] (aš, “food, meal”); Kazakh ас (as), Kyrgyz аш (aş), Crimean Tatar aş, Kumyk аш (aş), Uzbek osh (“hot meal; pilaf”), Azerbaijani aş, Turkish aş (“cooked food, meal”), Shor аш, Western Yugur as (“food”),
Yakut ас (as, “food, meal”), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *em (“medicine”). Compare to Salar em, Turkish em, Tuvan эм (em), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *ēr (“man”). Cognate with Kazakh ер (er), Kyrgyz эр (er), Crimean Tatar er, Karaim эр, Kumyk эр (er), Azerbaijani ər, Turkish er, Uzbek er, Shor эр,Tuvan эр (er), etc.
From Proto-Turkic *es (“memory, mind”). See above for more cognates.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *arkan. Compare to Kyrgyz аркан (arkan), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *bāka (“[uncertain meaning; possibilies include: frog or turtle/tortoise]”). Cognate with Old Turkic [script needed] (baka), Kazakh бақа (baqa), Kyrgyz бака (baka), Tatar бака (baqa), Bashkir баҡа (baqa), Tuvan пага (paga), Shor паға, Western Yugur paqa, Uyghur پاقا (paqa), Yakut баҕа (bağa), Turkish bağa, etc.

etymology_text: From Russian моне́та (monéta, “coin”), from Polish moneta, from Latin monēta. Cognate to Kumyk манат (manat), dialectal Tatar манат (manat), Azerbaijani manat, obsolete Turkish manat, Turkmen manat, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *Kum (“sand”). Cognate with Kazakh құм (qūm), Kyrgyz кум (kum), Crimean Tatar qum, Kumyk хум (xum), Bashkir ҡом (qom), Tatar ком (qom), Azerbaijani qum, Turkish kum, Turkmen gum, Uzbek qum, Khakas хум (xum), Shor қум, Tuvan кум (kum), Western Yugur qum, etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From алты (altï) + он (on).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *ogul (“son”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kār. Compare to Turkish kar.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kaŕgan.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *basa. Cognate to Kyrgyz баса (basa, “again”), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Compare to Kyrgyz бой (boy, “growth”)

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Compare to Kazakh кірпіш (kırpış), Turkish kerpiç, Bashkir кирбес (kirbes), etc.

etymology_text:
Adapted from Arabic قَلَعِيّ (qalaʕiyy, “related to tin”).
Cognate with Kazakh қалайы (qalaiy, “tin”), Uzbek qalay (“tin”), Azerbaijani qalay (“tin”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *(i)āka (“elder brother; uncle”). Cognate to Kyrgyz ага (aga), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kara (“black”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Arabic طَبَق (ṭabaq, “plate”). Cognate with Bashkir табаҡ (tabaq, “bowl”), Kyrgyz табак (tabak, “plate”), Uzbek tovoq (“bowl”), etc.

From Proto-Common Turkic *toy (“feast, banquet”). Cognate with Old Uyghur [script needed] (toj, “feast, banquet”), Crimean Tatar toy (“wedding”), Kumyk той (toy),
Kazakh той (toi), Nogai той (toy, “wedding”), Kyrgyz той (toy), Uzbek toʻy, Khakas той (toy), Turkish toy, Turkmen toý (“feast, banquet”), etc.
Relate to Kyrgyz той (toy), etc.

glosses: second-person singular imperative of тойор (toyor, “to full”)

etymology_text:

Cognate with Tatar сай (say, “shallow”),
Crimean Tatar say (“shallow place”),
Kumyk сай (say, “shallow”),
Khakas сай (say, “shallow place, shoal”).

From Proto-Turkic *say (“shallow; rocky bedding”).
Cognate with Tatar сай (say, “shallow”),
Crimean Tatar say (“shallow place”),
Kumyk сай (say, “shallow”),
Khakas сай (say, “shallow place, shoal”).
From Proto-Turkic *ōr. Cognate to Kazakh ор (or), etc.

examples.text: Алтай тилдиҥ кӱни баштапкы катап государственный окылу байрам болуп, 2014 јылдыҥ ӱлӱрген айыныҥ 20-чи кӱнинде, јарлу алтай бичиичи Лазарь Васильевич Кокышевтиҥ чыккан кӱнинде темдектелген.
examples.ref: 2025 October 14, “Алтай тилим, тӧрӧл тилим… [Altay tilim, töröl tilim…]”, in Национальная Библиотека Республики Алтай им. М. В. Чевалкова [Nacionalʹnaja Biblioteka Respubliki Altaj im. M. V. Čevalkova]:

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *yār-.
From Proto-Turkic *yār-.
From Proto-Turkic *yï̄lan (“snake”).

etymology_text: From earlier *ǰaztuq < *yaztuq, from Proto-Turkic *yaŕtuk (“pillow”). Cognate with Turkish yastık (“pillow”).

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *yumurtka, from јымырт (ǰïmïrt).

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kunduŕ (“beaver; otter”).
From ӧл- (öl-) + -ӱм (-üm).

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *banu. Cognate with Old Uyghur [script needed] (manu, “wild cat”), etc.

Southern Altai terms with redundant transliterations

etymology_text:
Compare to
Tatar мәшкә (mäşkä), мәшкәк (mäşkäk, “mushroom, fungus”),
Bashkir бәшмәк (bəşmək, “mushroom”),
Chulym мӓшкӓ (mäškä, “mushroom”),
Shor мешке (meške, “mushroom”),
Khakas миске (miske, “mushroom”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Borrowed from Written Oirat ᡏᡈᠨᡑᡈᠷ (möndör, “hail”), from Proto-Mongolic *möl-dür (“hail”). Compare Kyrgyz мөндүр (möndür), Shor мӱндӱр (mündür); Kalmyk мөндр (möndr), Buryat мүндэр (münder), Mongolian мөндөр (möndör).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Borrowed From Proto-Mongolic *meŋgün.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *kümüĺ. Cognate to Kyrgyz күмүш (kümüş), Kazakh күміс (kümıs), Nogai куьмис (kümis), Karakalpak gúmis, Crimean Tatar kümüş, Karaim kümüš, Kumyk гюмюш (gümüş), Bashkir көмөш (kömöş), Tatar көмеш (kömeş), Uzbek kumush, Uyghur كۈمۈش (kümüsh), Karakhanid كُمُشْ (kümüš, “silver”), Azerbaijani gümüş, Turkish gümüş, Old Turkic 𐰚𐰇𐰢𐱁 (kümüš, “silver”), Old Uyghur kwymwš (kümüš, “silver”), Northern Altai кӱмиш (kümiš), Shor кӱмӱш (kümüš), Khakas кӱмӱс (kümüs), Chuvash кӗмӗл (kĕmĕl, “silver”).

categories.name: Old Turkic terms with non-redundant manual transliterations
Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Russian морковь (morkovʹ).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: Compare to Kyrgyz момолой (momoloy), etc.

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text: From Proto-Turkic *büńüŕ (“horn”). Cognate with Bashkir мөгөҙ (mögöź, “horn”), Chuvash мӑйрака (măjrak̬a, “horn”), Turkish boynuz (“horn”).

categories.name: Southern Altai entries with incorrect language header

etymology_text:
From Proto-Turkic *agɨŕ. Cognate with
Old Turkic 𐰍𐰕 (ǧz /⁠aɣïz⁠/, “mouth”),
Khakas, Shor, Tuvan аас (aas),
Kazakh ауыз (auyz),
Kyrgyz ооз (ooz),
Bashkir ауыҙ (awıź),
Karachay-Balkar аууз (awuz),
Kumyk авуз (awuz),
Nogai, Tatar авыз (awız),
Uzbek ogʻiz,
Azerbaijani, Crimean Tatar, Turkish ağız,
Gagauz aaz, Turkmen agyz (“mouth”), etc.

categories.name: Karachay-Balkar terms with non-redundant manual transliterations
